Space layout: loft: open living/kitchen below, sleeping mezzanine + bathroom
Description: A small apartment / studio unit located in Shenzhen, China. The building footprint is approximately 11.8 m (east-west) by 6.6 m (north-south), rectangular, with the long axis rotated 75 degrees from north. Total gross floor area is about 77.5 m^2 spread over 1 floor(s), giving roughly 77.5 m^2 per floor. Floor-to-floor height is 2.9 m. The building is divided into 2 thermal zones in total (about 2 per floor). There are 1 dwelling units per floor (2 thermal zone(s) per unit), so model each unit as its own zone(s) stacked vertically across the 1 floor(s), plus a shared core/circulation. Typical space layout: loft: open living/kitchen below, sleeping mezzanine + bathroom. Window-to-wall ratio is approximately 20% on exterior walls, with windows distributed on all four facades. Model all zones with appropriate constructions, glazing, occupancy, lighting, equipment and HVAC (ideal loads) so the IDF runs in EnergyPlus without errors.
